original_prompt: When did Jesus establish the Holy Qurbono as the New Testament sacrifice? What was replaced?
canonical_answer:
On the day of the Last Supper
Animal sacrifice of the Old Testament
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What does the Holy Qurbono commemorate?
canonical_answer: Birth, public ministry, death, resurrection, and Second Coming of Jesus Christ
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What does the word ‘Qurbono’ mean?
canonical_answer: Offering of sacrifice
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is another name for Holy Qurbono/Holy Communion?
canonical_answer: Queen of Sacraments
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Holy Qurbono is the ‘___________’.
canonical_answer: Bread of life
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the 4 components of the Holy Qurbono?
canonical_answer:
The celebrant
The Sacramental items
Book of Holy Liturgy (Anaphora/Qurbono Taksa)
Recipient (congregation)
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Who is the celebrant and who is the ambassador?
canonical_answer:
God
Prelate (Patriarch/Catholicose/bishop) or priest
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the primary Sacramental items for the holy Qurbono?
canonical_answer: Leavened bread and wine mixed with water
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the official terms for the Book/Rite of the Holy Qurbono?
canonical_answer: Anaphora or Qurbono Taksa
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: How many different Taksas are there in existence?
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: The first church in Christianity is also known as _____________. What occurred here?
canonical_answer:
Upper Room of St. Mark
The Last Supper
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Who consecrates a church building? What is it anointed with?
canonical_answer:
A bishop
Holy Chrism (Mooron)
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the church building a symbol of?
canonical_answer: The presence of God among us
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the 3 main divisions of a church building?
canonical_answer:
Holy of the holies, the Sanctuary of the Madbaho
Chancel or Kestrumo
Nave or the Common Place
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Madbaho is from the root word dbah, meaning ‘_________’.
canonical_answer: To sacrifice
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: The Madbaho is the most sacred place in the church building. What does it represent?
canonical_answer: The Golgotha where the sacrifice of our Lord took place
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the thronos?
canonical_answer: The altar table located in the center of the sanctuary
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What does the Chancel/Kestrumo represent? What was offered here and when?
canonical_answer:
The space between the valley and the place where Moses prayed
Animal sacrifices in the Old Testament times
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What does the Nave/Common Place represent?
canonical_answer: The valley where the Israelites waited to receive blessing from God
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the veil called? What does it represent when it is opened?
canonical_answer:
Thirasheela
The opening of heaven
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What does thronos mean? What does it represent? What is another name for it?
canonical_answer:
Throne
The tomb of Christ
The Table of Life
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What does Sleebo mean? Where is it placed? What does it symbolize? It is described as the _________________.
canonical_answer:
Cross
In the middle of the thronos
The Lord’s sacrifice on Mount Golgotha
Weapon in the fight against Satan
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What do the 12 candles represent? What does the light from the candles symbolize?
canonical_answer:
The 12 apostles
The presence of God
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Candles made of animal fats permitted to use in the church. True or False?
canonical_answer: False
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the Tablitho? Why should it be handled carefully by a bishop, priest, or deacon? What does it have an inscription of?
canonical_answer:
A tablet/piece of wooden slab placed on the center of the thronos and covered with a cloth
Because it is anointed with Mooron
The details of the place, date, and by whom the consecration of it was done
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is another name for the Tablitho? Why is it called this?
canonical_answer:
Temporary altar
Holy Qurbono can be celebrated anywhere with it
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Without the Tablitho the Qurbono cannot be celebrated. True or False?
canonical_answer: True
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What does the Tablitho symbolize?
canonical_answer: The cross where Jesus was crucified
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: How many times can the Qurbono be celebrated on one Tablitho per day?
canonical_answer: Once
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is placed on the Tablitho during the Holy Qurbono?
canonical_answer: The paten and chalice
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the tricolor spread called? Describe it and what the colors represent.
canonical_answer:
Virikootam
Covers the Tablitho and made of red, green, and white/yellow pieces of cloth
Red = the universe, Green = the world, White/Yellow = the Holy Church
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the altar cloth called? What do the three parts symbolize?
canonical_answer:
Chithola
The Holy Trinity
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the altar step called? When does the celebrant stand on it and what does it symbolize?
canonical_answer:
Dargo
During the Anaphora (after the Nicene Creed)
Christ’s entrance into the Upper Room of St. Mark for the Last Supper
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the chalice and paten called? What do they hold?
canonical_answer:
Kasa and Peelaso
Kasa holds the wine and Peelaso holds the bread
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the purpose of the Kablana? When is it not used?
canonical_answer:
Used to cover the chalice and paten at beginning and close of service
When the chalice and paten are covered by the Shushefo
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: The Kablana symbolizes 2 things. What are they?
canonical_answer: The shroud that covered the body of Christ & the clouds that covered the tent in the desert during the plight of the Israelites from Egypt
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the Star (cross) called? Where is it placed? What does it symbolize?
canonical_answer:
Kaukbo
Over the paten
The Star of Nativity
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the bowl called? What is it used for?
canonical_answer:
Mshamshonitho
To mix the wine with water & for the priest to clean his fingers when handling the Holy Body and Blood
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the spoon called? What does it represent?
canonical_answer:
Tharvodo
The tongs that the angel used in giving the glowing coal to the mouth of prophet Isaiah in his vision
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the cushion called? What is it used for? What does it symbolize?
canonical_answer:
Gmurto
To clean the Sacramental vessels
The sponge used by the soldier to offer the sour wine to Christ while He was on the Cross
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the Shushefo used for? What does it represent?
canonical_answer:
To cover the paten and chalice
The fact that the Divine Mysteries are hidden from the understanding of men
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: The Shushefo symbolizes 2 things. What are they?
canonical_answer: The stone rolled over the Tomb of Christ & the rock of flint that sent 12 streams of water for the 12 tribes of Israel
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the Lectern? What does it symbolize? It is honored as the second ________.
canonical_answer:
Gospel Stand or Evengelion table
The heights of Mount Sinai where God spoke to Moses and handed him the Ten Commandment
Thronos
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the Censer called? How many chains and bells?
canonical_answer:
Dhoopakutty
4 chains and 12 bells
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What do the lower and upper cups of the censer represent? (Note: the lower cup represents 2 things)
canonical_answer:
Lower cup = the earth and the womb of St. Mary
Upper cup = heaven
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What does each chain of the censer represent?
canonical_answer:
1st chain = God the Father
2nd and 3rd chain = human and divine nature of God
4th chain = The Holy Spirit
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What do the bells of the censer represent?
canonical_answer: The 12 apostles
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What do the links on the chains of the censer represent?
canonical_answer: The saints, prophets, and emissaries who led the people of God
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What do the coal, fire, and incense of the censer represent?
canonical_answer:
Coal = the sinners
Fire = the Holy Spirit purifying the sinners to become children of God
Incense = the grace of the Holy Trinity and the cleansing of sins
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What do the altar assistants use the kai mani for?
canonical_answer: To proclaim and draw attention of the congregation to the important parts of the Qurbono
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the Church Bell used for and when? What should we do when we hear it ring?
canonical_answer:
To assemble the congregation to the Church for worship services and alerts people outside the church that the Holy Qurbono is about to start
Make the sign of the cross and say “Bless my Lord”
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Once the Holy Qurbono begins, the big Church bell should not ring. True or False?
canonical_answer: True
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: There are 3 other times when the Church bell is rung. What are they?
canonical_answer:
At the arrival of a bishop
During the procession for festivals
When a dead body is brought to the church for the funeral
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the fans called? What do they symbolize? What does their sound represent?
canonical_answer:
Marvahtho
The presence of angels around the altar
The seraphim and the fluttering of their wings around the mysteries
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the Lahmo? What is it made of?
canonical_answer:
Eucharistic bread
Wheat, flour, pure water, salt, leaven, and olive oil
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: The participants need to spend time in _________, ____________, and ______________ in order to be filled with thankfulness and repentance.
canonical_answer:
Prayer
Meditation
Reading the Holy scriptures
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Without _____________, we cannot get reconciled with God and dedicate ourselves to God’s will.
canonical_answer: Reconciliation with our brethren
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the 4 types of abstinence we should practice while preparing for the Qurbono?
canonical_answer:
Avoid worldly things and concentrate on Divine
Fasting
Physical Abstinence
Observance of Silence
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: When should the preparation for Holy Communion begin?
canonical_answer: On the previous evening
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: When should you begin to fast if you plan to receive Holy Communion?
canonical_answer: From midnight till you receive the Holy Communion
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What should you refrain from in order to prepare in fasting and prayer?
canonical_answer: All aspects of sexual activity
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use

original_prompt: Per the Canons of the Holy Church, we are advised to not talk to _________ before attending the Holy Qurbono. Hence it is better practice to _____________ as far as possible.
canonical_answer:
Non-Christians
Observe silence
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use

original_prompt: What is the name of the prayer the celebrant offers to those that are coming to receive Holy Communion?
canonical_answer: Hoosoyo
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: (In reference to the answer from #63) It replaces the Sacrament of Holy Confession. True or False?
canonical_answer: False
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use

original_prompt: Why do we take our shoes off before entering the Church?
canonical_answer: Because it is in reverence to the holiness of the place we are entering
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the prayer we say when we enter the Church?
canonical_answer: “With reverence will I enter thy house and offer my vows to Thee” (Psalm 61:8)
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: When is the dough for the Eucharistic bread prepared? When is it baked?
canonical_answer:
On the eve of the Holy Qurbono
On the same day of the Qurbono
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is the name of the set of preparatory prayers before the Holy Qurbono when the sanctuary is concealed by the veil?
canonical_answer: Thuyobo
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the 2 orders of priesthood, per the Holy Bible?
canonical_answer: The Order of Melchizedeck and the Order of Aaron
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: How is Malchizedeck described in the Bible?
canonical_answer: As the king of Salem and the priest of the most high God
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What is it called when the priest turns to the congregation and asks for forgiveness and prayers to make him worthy to offer the living sacrifice? When is the first time he does this?
canonical_answer:
Shubkono
During the Order of Melchizedeck (during morning prayer when he enters the Madbaho for the first time)
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: After the priest places the bread in paten, the chalice is prepared with equal water and wine. What does this represent?
canonical_answer: The low of blood and water from the side of Jesus when a soldier pierced His side while on the cross
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What does the Order of Aaron symbolize?
canonical_answer: The offerings of Aaron and his sons as mentioned in Leviticus 1
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: When the celebrant covers the paten and chalice with the Shushefo, he censes the mysteries 3 times at the center, 3 times on the left, and 3 times on the right. When he censes on the left, he ____________.
canonical_answer: Folds the corner of the Shushefo saying ‘Son died on the cross’
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: How is the Order of Aaron concluded?
canonical_answer: With the huthomo, quamo, and the Nicene Creed
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the names of the 2 parts of the Public Celebration of the Holy Qurbono?
canonical_answer: Mass of the Catechumens and the Anaphora of the faithful
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: As part of the Holy Qurbono, the Church provides the opportunity for special prayers. Name the 4 categories of special prayers. When are they remembered?
canonical_answer:
Vazhipadkurbana, intercession, sickness, and departed
During the Thuyobo
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: Who was the Mass of the Catechumens primarily intended for?
canonical_answer: New believers and those who were not yet baptized
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does the Mass of the Catechumens consist of?
canonical_answer: Procession around the thronos, the Trisagion, the reading from the epistle, and the Gospel and sermon
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does the opening of the Thirasheela represent at the start of the Public Celebration?
canonical_answer: The birth of the New Testament age and opening of heaven to the faithful
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Who do the celebrant and deacon/thurifer holding the candle represent when they move in a procession around the thronos? (after “Mariyam Dheelethok…”)
canonical_answer: Christ the High Priest and John the Baptist who prepared the way for our Lord
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does this procession signify? What does the censing around the altar symbolize?
canonical_answer:
The public ministry of our Lord across the 4 corners of the earth
The purification of the altar
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: Who heard the angels sing the Trisagion and when/where?
canonical_answer:
Nicodemus and Joseph of Aramathia
When they took the body of Christ for burial/ during the crucifixion
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: During the lections (Bible readings) the deacon/altar server steps down to the chancel step. What does this and the readings at the north and south sides symbolize?
canonical_answer:
The fact that the Apostles were sent by heavenly authority
The fact that they spread the Gospel in all corners of the world
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does the word ‘Habibai’ mean?
canonical_answer: My beloved
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does the word ‘Ahai’ mean?
canonical_answer: My brethren
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What do the lit candles during the Gospel reading represent?
canonical_answer: The Cherubim with flaming swords
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What are the rites included in the Pre-Anaphora?
canonical_answer: Promion, Hoosoyo, Sedro, blessing of the censer, and the Nicene Creed
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does ‘Staumen kalos’ mean?
canonical_answer: Let us stand well OR be attentive
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does ‘Kuriyelaison’ mean?
canonical_answer: Lord have mercy upon us
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the importance of the Promion?
canonical_answer: It remembers the great sacrifice of our Lord and asks him to make us worthy of offering the sacrifice
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: After the Promion, the celebrant places incense and unfolds the left corner of the Shushefo. What does he say when he does this and what does it symbolize?
canonical_answer:
“Christ risen from the tomb”
The revelation of the resurrected Christ to humanity
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What prayer follows the Promion? What is the importance of it?
canonical_answer:
Hoosoyo
The celebrant asks the Lord for mercy and absolution on behalf of the congregation
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the long prayer reflecting on the fundamental tenets of our faith and on the greatness of the divine Qurbono called?
canonical_answer: Sedro
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: The blessing of the censer is also a ______________.
canonical_answer: Confession of the Holy Trinity
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: Describe which part of the Qurbono the celebrant asks for Shubkono for the SECOND time.
canonical_answer: After the blessing of the censor/during the Nicene Creed/before the Anaphora
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: When was the Nicene Creed formulated, where, and by whom?
canonical_answer: In 325 AD at the Council of Nicea, by the 318 Fathers of the early Church
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: The Nicene Creed was formulated after the Church determined the New Testament. True or False?
canonical_answer: False
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use

original_prompt: What are the major tenets of the Nicene Creed?
canonical_answer:
Holy Trinity
Holy Church
Baptism
Final resurrection
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the 4 fundamental characteristics of the Holy Church, as written in the Nicene Creed?
canonical_answer:
One
Holy
Catholic
Apostolic
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does the term ‘Anaphora’ mean?
canonical_answer: Offering
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does the celebrant do that signifies the start of the Anaphora?
canonical_answer: He steps on to the Dargo, representing the Lord’s entrance into the Upper Room of St. Mark for the Last Supper
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: Where did the Church adopt the tradition of the Kiss of Peace?
canonical_answer: From the Jewish practice of kissing/hugging one another before a meal
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: The Kiss of Peace also fulfills the words of our Lord from His ______________.
canonical_answer: Sermon on the Mount
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: The celebration of the Shushefo is symbolic of ___________’s vision at the city of Joppa. What did the vision tell this person?
canonical_answer:
St. Peter
That the grace of God is open to all who confess their faith in Christ
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What does the celebration of the Shushefo also represent? (Hint: Jesus’ baptism)
canonical_answer: The opening of heaven and the descent of the Holy Spirit in the form of a dove
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the first benediction also known as?
canonical_answer: The Apostolic Benediction
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the most important part of the Qurbono known as?
canonical_answer: The Words of Institution
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the meaning of “a’damo doseno”?
canonical_answer: Until I come
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: When the celebrant says “a’damo doseno,” he raises the Tharvodo and Gmurto over his right shoulder in a quick motion. What does this represent?
canonical_answer: The second coming of Christ on the last day, which will be like a flash of lightning
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the celebrant asking for during the Invocation of the Holy Spirit?
canonical_answer: That God the Father would send down the Holy Spirit upon the bread and wine
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: When the celebrant raises his hands and cries out “Answer me, O Lord” 3x, it is similar to which other invocation from the Bible?
canonical_answer: Prophet Elijah’s at Mount Carmel
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: Why is the Invocation of the Holy Spirit also another most important part of the Qurbono?
canonical_answer: Because this is when the bread and wine truly become the body and blood of Christ
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does the word ‘thubden’ mean?
canonical_answer: Again
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: Who do the first 3 thubdens remember? Name them all.
canonical_answer:
The living
The spiritual fathers who tend the Church, the living faithful brethren, and the living faithful rulers
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: Who do the last 3 thubdens remember? Name them all.
canonical_answer:
The departed
Intercession of the Mother of God and saints, the fathers of the Church, and the departed faithful
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the importance of the second benediction?
canonical_answer: The blessing is by the God our Saviour Jesus Christ instead of the Holy Trinity as in the other 2 benedictions
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: During the song ‘Anpudayone’, what does the closed veil remind us of?
canonical_answer: The time of Christ’s redemptive passion, death, burial, and resurrection, when the earth was engulfed in darkness
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the importance of the Fracture & Commixture?
canonical_answer: This is the part where the celebrant commemorates the passion, crucifixion, and resurrection of our Lord
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: During ‘Anpudayone’, we hear the bells being rung. What is the significance?
canonical_answer: The resurrection of Christ
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What are the 3 different patterns of breaking the bread? Explain each one.
canonical_answer:
Lamb pattern: used from Koodash Etho to Easter, Maundy Thursday, and Holy Saturday
Crucifix Pattern: human; used from Easter to the Feast of the Holy Cross
Cross Pattern: used from the Feast of the Cross to Koodash Etho
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: After the Fracture & Commixture, what is the importance of the veil being opened?
canonical_answer:
This part of the Qurbono corresponds to the days between the resurrection and ascension
The Old Testament priesthood, which was restricted to one tribe, was opened to all through the sacrifice of Christ, revealed through the tearing of the veil in the Jerusalem Temple
Man was brought close to God again, where they could call God the ‘Father’ through Christ’s sacrifice at Golgotha
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does the third benediction symbolize?
canonical_answer: The blessing that our Lord gave to His disciples before His ascension
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What does the Elevation of the Holy Mysteries celebrate?
canonical_answer: The ascension of our Lord
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Afterwards, the celebrant covers places the paten and chalice on the Tablitho and covers them with the Kablana. What does this represent?
canonical_answer: The Kablana represents the cloud that hid Christ during His ascension
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: When is the THIRD time the celebrant asks for Shubkono?
canonical_answer: After the Elevation of the Holy Mysteries and during the Hymns of Eucharistic Devotion
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What song can the Hymns of Eucharistic devotion be replaced with?
canonical_answer: “Bakyamthe d’malko…”
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What are the 4 categories for the Hymns of Eucharistic devotion?
canonical_answer:
Intercession of St. Mary
Intercession of the Saints
Prayers for the departed Clergy
Prayers for the departed faithful
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy

canonical_answer:
Quaqal’yon (Kukkilion)
Eqbo

review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: Practice singing and writing the 4 Kukkilions (English and Malayalam/Manglish).
canonical_answer:
Intercession to St. Mary:
See the royal daughter’s stand
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
Glorious queen at thy right hand
Thy father’s folk and home leave thou
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
The king desires the beauty now
Barekhmor
//
Ninnal suthiyodu rajamakal
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
Nin valabhage raja bhaminiyum
Ninazhakarachan mohipan
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
Nin janamorkayka pithrugrahavum
Barekhmor
Intercession to the Saints:
The righteous shall prosper like palm trees
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
And thrive like the cedars of Lebanon
In age they shall thrive and be flourishing
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
Yea, growing both fattened and pleasing
Barekhmor
//
Nayavan panapole thalarthidume
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
Valarumavan Lebanon kaarakilpol
Vruthathayilumavar thalirittu thazhachidume
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
Vardhikkumavarkku thushti pushtikalum
Barekhmor
Prayers for the Departed Clergy:
The priests be clothed in righteousness and thy saints in glory
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
For the sake of David, your servant
If thy children will keep my covenant
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
And the statutes that I teach
Barekhmor
//
Chaarthum neethiye ninnacharyanmaarum mahimaye nin nayavanmarum
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
Ninnabhishiktha mukham thirippikalle nin daasan Daveedin moolam
Nin puthranmaar kaathidu menkil
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
En niyametheyum mjanupadesichidumee saakshiyeyum
Barekhmor
Prayers for the Faithful Departed:
As doth a father his children love
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
So does the Lord love those who fear His name
The days of man are but as grass
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
He springs up like the herbs that grow in the fields
Barekhmor
//
Makkalilappen krupa cheyvathupole
Halleluiah ou Halleluiah
Bhakthanmaaril deivam krupa cheyyum
Pullinu thulyam naranude naalkalaho
Halleluah ou Halleluiah
Pookkunithu vayalil poo-chedipole
Barekhmor
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: Before the Procession of the Holy Mysteries, the veil is closed. What does this symbolize?
canonical_answer: The age in which the Church awaits the second coming of our Lord
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: At the start of the Procession of the Holy Mysteries, the veil is opened. What does this represent?
canonical_answer: The glorious second coming of our Lord
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the significance of the celebrant turning counterclockwise to face the congregation?
canonical_answer: That the Lord is coming as a judge this time, which is in contrast of the first coming when He came as Redeemer
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: When the celebrant stretches out his hands to introduce the Holy Body ad Holy Blood one after the other, he pleads to the Lord that the reception of the Holy Mysteries be for _______ and _______ and not for any ________ or ________.
canonical_answer:
Life
Redemption
Punishment
Condemnation
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the purpose of the Prayer of Thanksgiving?
canonical_answer: For the celebrant to offer praise and express thanks to the Father for making him and the congregation worthy to partake in the heavenly feast, which is like a joyful wedding banquet
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: When the celebrant greets the congregation with peace for the last time, it is representing ____________.
canonical_answer: The eternal peace offered to the holy people at the Second Coming of our Lord
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: What is the name of the concluding prayer done by the celebrant?
canonical_answer: Hoothomo
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: After the benediction, the celebrant steps down and kneels before the thronos for a silent prayer. The veil is drawn to close the sanctuary. What is the significance of this?
canonical_answer: It denotes the conclusion of the Public Celebration of the Holy Qurbono
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: Why is the final benediction so important?
canonical_answer: It acknowledges the presence of all who are ‘far and near’, ‘living and departed’, ‘who are saved by the victorious cross’ and ‘who are inscribed with the seal of Holy Baptism’ during the Holy Qurbono and that the blessing is for all those who are participating in it physically or spiritually
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use

category: Order and Meaning of the Liturgy
original_prompt: After the post-communion prayers, how does the celebrant take leave of the altar?
canonical_answer: By stepping down from the Dargo and kissing the Tablitho to the middle to the left and to the right saying the three prayers of farewell with a contrite heart
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Name the Ten Commandments.
canonical_answer:
You shall have no other gods before me
You shall not make for yourself a graven image, or any likeness of anything that is in heaven above or that is in earth beneath, or that is in the water underneath the earth. You shall not bow down to them or serve them
You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain
Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy
Honor your mother and father, that your days may be long in the land which the Lord your God gives you.
You shall not kill
You shall not commit adultery
You shall not steal
You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor
You shall not covet your neighbor’s house or anything that is your neighbor’s
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: What are the 7 Laws of the Holy Church?
canonical_answer:
Attend the Holy Qurbono on Sundays and other obligatory days
Observe the 5 Lents of the Church
Observe the Lent on Wednesdays and Fridays
Confess the sins before a canonical priest
Receive the Holy Communion
Abstain from marital relationship on prescribed days
Pay offerings, tithes, and duties to the church
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use

original_prompt: Where did the 3 Ecumenical Synods take place (and the importance of each--just the overall importance)?
canonical_answer:
Nicea: Father and Son
Constantinople: Holy Spirit
Ephesus: Holy Virgin Mother
review_focus: Confirm wording and local teaching preference

original_prompt: Practice saying and writing the prayer before receiving Holy Communion.
canonical_answer: My God and my Lord, make me worthy to receive Thy Holy Body and life-giving precious blood for the eradication of my evil desires, and for the expulsion of sinful deeds. Help me O God, through partaking in these Holy Mysteries, for the remission of my debts, and forgiveness of my sins; and for the purification of my body and the enlightenment of my soul into life everlasting. Amen.
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use

original_prompt: Practice saying and writing the prayer after receiving Holy Communion.
canonical_answer: O Lord Jesus Christ, our God, I have received Thy Holy Body and precious Blood unto remission of sins and for life eternal. May this Eucharist be unto me not for condemnation, but for the life and redemption; for joy, health, and gladness; and to make me worthy to stand at Thy right hand during Thy second coming. Amen.
review_focus: Local clergy review required before use
